notes: MIF-006 rate decoding is measured for Python and Rust runtime surfaces plus a Julia polyglot-parity reference over the committed 256-event AER fixture; production stays on the Rust runtime path.

Event timestamps, explicit window starts, and decode-window lengths are bounded to the shared u64 nanosecond domain on the Python and Rust/PyO3 surfaces; u64 stop overflow fails closed. The Julia parity reference uses Int64 timestamps and reproduces the rate-decode mathematics (channel 0 = 8 / 4096).
Julia is measured through the package CLI harness; the recorded timing includes Julia process startup, so it ranks last and is a comparison reference, not a runtime path.
Non-isolated local comparison run under taskset affinity and heavy host load; do not treat as a production performance claim.

benchmark_context.isolation_method: taskset affinity to logical cores 8-11; no kernel isolcpus reservation
benchmark_context.heavy_jobs_running: shared workstation under heavy contention; absolute timings are noisy and the Julia CLI timing is dominated by process startup. The fastest-first ranking (rust < python < julia) is robust to this load because the backends differ by orders of magnitude.
benchmark_context.hardware_summary: x86_64, 12 logical CPUs, 6 cores, 2 threads per core
